case_title: DEPARTMENT OF INDUSTRIAL RELATIONS VS. BAY AREA RAPID TRANSIT DISTRICT

docket_entries.description: REPLY BRIEF IN SUPPORT OF ITS MTN TO VACATE JUDGMENT FILED BY DEFENDANT BAY AREA RAPID TRANSIT DISTRICT (BART)

LAW AND MOTION 302, NO APPEARANCE. THE COURT MODIFIED ITS TENTATIVE RULING PURSUANT TO PARTIES' REQUEST. DEFENDANT BAY AREA RAPID TRANSIT DISTRICT'S MOTION TO VACATE JUDGMENT IS CONTINUED TO MARCH 26, 2014 AT 9:30 AM IN DEPARTMENT 302. (THE COURT NOW HAS COURTESY COPIES OF THE MOVING, OPPOSITION AND REPLY PAPERS.) JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR# 5976.

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT BAY AREA RAPID TRANSIT DISTRICT'S (BART) MOTION TO VACATE JUDGMENT. RULING: ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING: GRANTED. THE CLERK PREMATURELY ENTERED THE JUDGMENT BECAUSE WHETHER PLAINTIFF'S CITATION/PENALTY WAS PROPERLY ISSUED IS CURRENTLY BEING LITIGATED IN SACRAMENTO BY A PETITION FOR WRIT OF ADMINISTRATIVE MANDAMUS. JUDGMENT SHOULD BE ENTERED AFTER DEFENDANT'S REMEDIES ARE EXHAUSTED. SEE LABOR CODE SEC. 6650(B). ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR5976

calendar.matters:
Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Vacate Judgment
Off Calendar Mar-26-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Allyce Kimerling, Staff Counsel, 510.286.7348, for plaintiff Department of Industrial Relations.
Morgan Lopez, Esq., 925.210.2808, for defendant Bay Area Rapid Transit District.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling: DEFENDANT BAY AREA RAPID TRANSIT DISTRICT'S (BART) Motion To Vacate Judgment is granted. The clerk prematurely entered the judgment because whether plaintiff's citation/penalty was properly issued is currently being litigated in Sacramento by a petition for writ of administrative mandamus. Judgment should be entered after defendant's remedies are exhausted. See Labor Code sec. 6650(b). Order signed in open court. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR5976

Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Vacate Judgment
Off Calendar Mar-11-2014 Continued To Mar-26-14 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance. The Court modified its tentative ruling pursuant to parties' request. DEFENDANT BAY AREA RAPID TRANSIT DISTRICT's Motion To Vacate Judgment is continued to March 26, 2014 at 9:30 am in Department 302. (The court now has courtesy copies of the moving, opposition and reply papers.) Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR# 5976.
